content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward signals from ICM/RND curious agents can be repurposed as a dynamic regularization gate for online meta-learning memory consolidation, where high intrinsic prediction error (the same signal that drives exploration) marks recently learned task parameters as high-priority for protection against catastrophic forgetting. This creates a self-supervised cycle: the agent’s own drive to explore unfamiliar new tasks automatically identifies which new memories to preserve when adapting to continually changing distribution.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
Online meta-learning memory consolidation with catastrophic forgetting mitigation
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
